commands.review_summary:
The PR moves internal tool/agent trace-line stripping into the shared assistant-visible sanitizer, keeps Discord-specific channel-label stripping at the send boundary, and updates Discord/process/sanitizer tests.

PR surface: Source +47, Tests +56. Total +103 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: current main's Discord process tests expect pure tool-warning finals to be delivered in fallback cases, and current reply-safety strips internal trace text only at the Discord send boundary. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor proof or a maintainer proof override is needed; no narrow automated code repair remains from this read-only review.

target.title: Suppress internal agent failure traces before channel delivery
target.body:
## Summary

- Move internal agent/tool trace-line stripping into the shared assistant-visible text sanitizer so non-Discord delivery users get the same suppression for failed-tool payloads.
- Preserve Discord's stricter send-boundary guard for plain internal channel labels such as `analysis:`, `commentary:`, `thinking:`, and `reasoning:`.
- Strip trace-only error-context payloads before user-facing delivery while preserving explicit tool-progress payloads and non-text Discord payloads.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/reply-delivery.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts -t "tool progress"`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Internal agent/tool failure trace lines such as `⚠️ 🛠️ \`run ... (agent)\` failed` are no longer delivered as user-visible Discord messages when they are the only final text, and the generic assistant-visible sanitizer now removes the same trace shape for other delivery users. Discord delivery also continues to suppress plain internal channel-label lines such as `analysis:` and `commentary:` while still sending media, channelData, and presentation payloads when their text scrubs empty.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on current `origin/main` after rebasing and follow-up commit `63376624dd`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/reply-delivery.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts -t "tool progress"`; `git diff --check`.

Evidence after fix: Shared sanitizer tests strip warning/tool trace lines and preserve fenced examples and ordinary `Analysis:` headings; Discord delivery tests drop pure internal warning payloads and restored `analysis:`/`commentary:` channel-label payloads while preserving media/rich payloads when text scrubs empty; process tests confirm warning finals are suppressed instead of sent.

Observed result after fix: Focused sanitizer and Discord tests passed; the tool-progress regression test passed with 4 matching tests and 63 skipped by filter; whitespace diff check passed.

What was not tested: Live Discord delivery was not exercised in this checkout. The remaining Real behavior proof gate still needs redacted live Discord/Mantis evidence or maintainer override before merge.
